observed_evidence: {"symptom_reproduction":"Independently re-ran the shipped D1 real-path entrypoint `probes/real_path_probe.py --phase d1` with `.venv/bin/python` (real transformers/torch install, DEKU_WORKER_MODE=local_double), own scratch dir /tmp/qa-d1-probe (not the builder's tmp scratch under /var/folders/.../implementer). Reproduced identical shape: real Nemotron-8B weight load (~21-28s, visible checkpoint-shard progress bar), healthz/models schema correct (context_window=40960, truncation_policy.limit=40960), hello soak non-blank, multi-turn non-blank."}

{"own_probes":"Wrote and ran an independent Python driver (not the builder's fixture) hitting `/v1/responses` directly through deku_server's FastAPI TestClient: (a) sent 'hello' and timed the call, (b) sent a non-greeting, non-canned-pattern query ('Tell me a fun fact about octopus camouflage biology in detail') and timed that call, to isolate whether real Nemotron inference is actually exercised per request."}

{"real_entry_surface":"Drove the real shipped HTTP surface (`POST /v1/responses` via deku_server.app, the same entrypoint codex/model_provider=deku hits) and the real `/healthz`, `/v1/models` handlers, plus `python -m unittest discover -s tests -v` with `.venv/bin/python` (deku_core-repo local suite, not brick_protocol tests)."}

{"finding":"Greeting-path bypasses the loaded Nemotron model entirely. `deku_core/strategy.py:77-80 is_greeting()` matches '\uc548\ub155'/'hello' (both used in D1 hello soak); `deku_server.py:163-164` sets `strategy_id='greeting'` for any greeting; `deku_core/conduct.py:93-98` (greeting branch) never calls `real_run` \u2014 it calls `worker(slot, prompt)` directly, and worker is `dispatch_worker` which under `DEKU_WORKER_MODE=local_double` (`deku.py:54-71`) resolves to `mock_worker` (`deku_core/conduct.py:33-49`), a hardcoded string-match function. Own probe: greeting call elapsed 0.01s (vs ~21-28s model load), and returned the byte-identical canned string '\uc548\ub155\ud558\uc138\uc694. Deku-1 mock worker\uc785\ub2c8\ub2e4. \ubb34\uc5c7\uc744 \ub3c4\uc640\ub4dc\ub9b4\uae4c\uc694?' on every single one of the builder's 20 hellos (verified by reading d1-hellos.json) and on my own reruns (n=3)."}

{"finding":"Non-greeting path does invoke real Nemotron inference (own probe: 140.18s wall-clock for one non-greeting request, consistent with `deku.py:153-239 run()` calling `model.generate()` under a mutex across up to MAX_ROUNDS=6 steps on MPS) \u2014 but the *final user-facing answer text* is still produced by `WORKER[slot](wp)` -> `dispatch_worker` -> `mock_worker` in local_double mode, not by the model. Own probe with a query matching none of mock_worker's canned branches returned the literal fallback template `f\"[mock:{slot}] processed: {prompt[-200:]}\"` (observed: `[mock:search-1] processed: uflage biology in detail\\n\\nReason step by step...`), i.e. an echo of the prompt tail with a '[mock:...]' prefix, not generated content. The builder's multi-turn evidence (`d1-multiturn.json`: a1='408', a2='408') matches the hardcoded `mock_worker` branch `if \"17\" in p and \"24\" in p: return \"408\"` (`deku_core/conduct.py:42-43`) verbatim rather than any model computation."}

{"finding":"`/healthz`'s `loaded` field (`deku_server.py:487-495`) only checks `_MODEL is not None` \u2014 this part of the claim is accurate: the real 8B checkpoint (`artifacts/nemotron-8b`) is genuinely loaded via `transformers.AutoModelForCausalLM`/`AutoTokenizer` (confirmed by the visible shard-loading progress bar and ~21-28s load time), not faked."}

{"finding":"unittest claim reproduced: `.venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -v` in /Users/smith/projects/deku -> 'Ran 27 tests in 0.265s / OK', matching the builder's recorded regression.txt ('Ran 27 tests in 0.278s / OK')."}

{"finding":"The first probe attempt in the builder's own commands_run failed with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'transformers'` (d1-load-env.txt, d1-probe-stdout.txt, both timestamped 22:48:33) because it was not run with `.venv/bin/python`; the builder's later successful run explicitly switched to `/Users/smith/projects/deku/.venv/bin/python` (confirmed `.venv` has `transformers==5.13.0`, `torch==2.12.1` installed) \u2014 the switch is a real environment fix, not evidence tampering."}

{"finding":"Building checker profile `check_profile.py --all` run fresh from this Building's W1 worktree cwd: all listed profiles report 'passed' (no failures observed) \u2014 general BRICK kernel health only, does not touch deku-repo-specific behavior."}

{"finding":"deku repo git state: deku.py and deku_server.py are modified relative to `git HEAD` (98 and 556 diff lines respectively, uncommitted); deku_core/, probes/, tests/, vendor/ are entirely untracked (`??`). The upstream work step-output nonetheless reports `made_changes: false, changed_files: []` \u2014 internally consistent only because this Brick's declared write_scope is the BRICK worktree, not `/Users/smith/projects/deku` (matches the previously recorded 'fake_landing_write_scope_diff_absent' lifecycle note in docs/DEKU_STATUS.md), but it means none of this D1 implementation is captured by any git diff artifact this or any future QA/closure lane can inspect as a durable record."}

proof_limits: Agent hook:reviewer-no-mutation blocks source_write while allowing declared work-area probe_write
source_write vs probe_write is prompt/evidence disciplined here, not a filesystem-enforced provider distinction
regression_risks: Non-greeting real-path requests measured at ~140s wall-clock for a single turn (own probe) on MPS — a severe, previously-uncalled-out latency risk if this path is ever hit by a real user session (D2 phase does 14+ chained turns, which at this per-turn cost would be tens of minutes).

The greeting fast-path silently short-circuits the entire conductor for any input matching `is_greeting()` (single-word/short greeting), so any future 'expand real-path coverage' work risks assuming hello-soak evidence exercises the conductor when it structurally cannot.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence
